title: inconsistent results on signed operations
body:
Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)
```verilog
module test();
  reg clock;
  initial begin
    clock = 0;
  end
  always #1 clock = ~clock;

  wire [63:0] in1 = 64'hfffffffbffffffff;
  wire [63:0] in2 = 64'hffffffffffffffdf;

  wire [63:0] div_data = $signed(in1) / $signed(in2);
  wire [63:0] result1 =
    in2 == 64'h0 ? 64'hFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF :
      div_data;

  wire [63:0] result2 =
    in2 == 64'h0 ? 64'hFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF :
      $signed(in1) / $signed(in2);

  always @(posedge clock) begin
    if (result1 != result2) begin
      $display("div_data: %x, $signed(in1) / $signed(in2): %x",
        div_data, $signed(in1) / $signed(in2));
      $display("result1: %x, result2: %x", result1, result2);
      $error();
    end
  end

endmodule
```

`result1` and `result2` are expected to be the same, is it? However, it says
```
div_data: 000000001f07c1f0, $signed(in1) / $signed(in2): 000000001f07c1f0
result1: 000000001f07c1f0, result2: 0000000000000000
[1] %Error: test.v:27: Assertion failed in TOP.test
%Error: test.v:27: Verilog $stop
Aborting...
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
`verilator --cc --exe --main --timing test.v`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
`Verilator 5.021 devel rev v5.020-22-g19cb366e5`. It's the latest master branch.

What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 20.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I'm not sure whether this is a bug or feature. I tried O0-O3 but all version produces the same result. I'm not having VCS installed now but I could have a try in the next days if needed.
